operating_state: Total revenues increased 6.0% to $648.1 billion driven by positive comparable sales across all segments, with U.S. comparable sales up 4.9% and international markets showing broad strength. Walmart U.S. comparable sales rose 5.5% driven by transaction growth and higher average ticket in grocery and health and wellness, with eCommerce contributing significantly. Sam's Club comparable sales increased despite fuel deflation headwinds, benefiting from membership growth and Plus penetration expansion.
cost_structure: Gross profit rate improved 27 basis points to 23.7% primarily from Walmart U.S. managing competitive price gaps and lapping prior year markdowns, partially offset by product mix shifts to lower margin categories. Operating expenses as a percentage of sales decreased 60 basis points, benefiting from lapping opioid settlement charges and international restructuring costs from the prior year. Operating income margin expanded 83 basis points to 4.2%.
material_events: Board authorized and executed 3-for-1 stock split effective February 2024, increasing authorized shares from 11 billion to 33 billion while maintaining par value. Company acquired Flipkart noncontrolling interests and settled PhonePe liabilities while receiving funding from new PhonePe equity. Annual dividend increased to $0.83 per share from $0.76, representing 9.2% increase. Two senior executives established Rule 10b5-1 trading plans for systematic stock sales extending through 2025-2026.
macro_exposures: Merchandise costs continued experiencing inflation impacts at lower rates than fiscal 2023, with pricing strategies including selective cost absorption and pass-through decisions affecting gross margins. Currency exchange rates provided positive impact to international net sales during fiscal 2024. Supply chain pressures and macroeconomic volatility remain ongoing concerns affecting consumer confidence and employment trends.
forward_outlook: Capital expenditures projected at $20-24 billion for fiscal 2025 focused on technology, supply chain and customer-facing initiatives. Management committed to financial framework prioritizing growth through omni-channel expansion, margin improvement via productivity initiatives and business mix optimization, and enhanced returns through disciplined capital allocation. Strategic focus continues on automation investments and connected value streams including advertising and membership income.
uncertainty_profile: Working capital deficit reflects efficient cash utilization model with consistent capital market access supporting operations and shareholder returns. Free cash flow generation of $15.1 billion provides flexibility for strategic investments and capital allocation priorities. Credit ratings remain strong across agencies supporting favorable refinancing capabilities.
